title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

seo.imageAlt: Perfectly seared New York Strip Steak with melted garlic butter, fresh herbs, and a golden crust.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in a restaurant-quality New York Strip Steak with rich garlic butter, made easily at home.

description: Experience the ultimate American steakhouse flavor at home with this New York Strip Steak recipe, perfectly seared and basted with aromatic garlic butter. Each bite offers a tender, juicy interior with a beautiful caramelized crust, creating an unforgettable dinner. This dish is a true celebration of rich, savory flavors that will impress any palate.

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Pat the New York Strip steaks thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season generously on all sides with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Let them sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before cooking.

instructions.title: Heat the Skillet
instructions.text: Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat until it just begins to smoke. Add the olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom of the pan.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep brown, caramelized crust forms. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook one steak at a time if necessary.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Butter
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the cold butter cubes, smashed garlic cloves, and fresh rosemary and thyme sprigs to the pan. The butter should melt and foam around the steaks.

instructions.title: Baste and Cook to Perfection
instructions.text: Tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to continuously baste the melting garlic herb butter over the steaks. Continue cooking and basting for another 3-5 minutes, flipping the steaks every minute, until they reach your desired internal temperature (e.g., 130-135°F for medium-rare, 135-140°F for medium).

instructions.title: Rest the Steaks
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked steaks to a clean cutting board. Pour any remaining pan drippings and garlic butter from the skillet over them. Loosely tent the steaks with foil and let them rest for at least 10 minutes before slicing. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and flavorful steak.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: Slice the rested steaks against the grain into thick, even pieces. Drizzle with any accumulated juices from the cutting board and serve immediately with your favorite American dinner sides.

tips: Ensure your steak is at room temperature before searing for an even cook and better crust development, a classic American steakhouse technique.
For an extra depth of flavor, consider adding a dash of Worcestershire sauce or a pinch of smoked paprika to your seasoning blend before searing.
Always rest your steak for at least 10 minutes after cooking. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and flavorful cut.
Use a high smoke point oil like avocado or grapeseed oil for searing to achieve a perfect crust without burning the oil.
article:
The sizzle of a perfectly seared steak, the intoxicating aroma of garlic and butter, and the promise of a truly satisfying meal – these are the hallmarks of a classic American dinner. Few dishes embody this culinary dream quite like a New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter. This isn't just a meal; it's an experience, a celebration of rich flavors and tender textures that can transform an ordinary evening into something special. Simple yet elegant, this recipe brings gourmet restaurant quality right to your kitchen, making it an ideal choice for a memorable dinner at home.

## About New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter

The New York Strip, also known as Kansas City Strip, ambassador steak, or strip loin, is a beloved cut for good reason. Taken from the short loin of the cow, it boasts a fine grain, a good amount of marbling, and a robust beefy flavor that stands up beautifully to bold seasonings. It’s tender without being overly fatty, striking a perfect balance between succulence and chew. Its consistent shape and thickness make it ideal for pan-searing, allowing for an even cook and that coveted golden-brown crust.

When paired with garlic butter, the New York Strip reaches new heights. The butter, infused with aromatic garlic and often fresh herbs like rosemary or thyme, melts into the steak as it cooks, creating a flavorful basting liquid. This process not only adds incredible depth of flavor but also helps to keep the steak moist and tender. The result is a steak with a deeply caramelized exterior and a juicy, flavorful interior, making New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter a quintessential American dinner recipe cherished for its straightforward deliciousness.

## Ingredients for New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

Crafting this magnificent dish requires a few high-quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need to create an unforgettable New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter:

*   **New York Strip Steaks:** Two 1-1.5 inch thick steaks (about 12-16 oz each). Choose steaks with good marbling.
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 6 tablespoons. Unsalted allows you to control the seasoning precisely.
*   **Garlic:** 4-6 cloves, minced or thinly sliced. Fresh garlic is non-negotiable for flavor.
*   **Fresh Rosemary or Thyme:** 2-3 sprigs. These herbs infuse the butter with an earthy, aromatic quality.
*   **Kosher Salt:** 1-2 teaspoons, or to taste. Essential for drawing out moisture and enhancing flavor.
*   **Freshly Ground Black Pepper:** 1 teaspoon, or to taste. Adds a subtle bite.
*   **High Smoke Point Oil:** 1-2 tablespoons (e.g., avocado oil, grapeseed oil, or canola oil). Crucial for achieving a beautiful sear without burning.

## How to Make New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

The process for cooking a perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is straightforward, but attention to detail makes all the difference.

1.  **Prepare the Steaks:** Remove the steaks from the refrigerator at least 30-60 minutes before cooking to allow them to come to room temperature. This promotes even cooking. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels; excess moisture prevents a good sear. Season generously on all sides with kosher salt and black pepper. Don’t be shy with the seasoning!
2.  **Heat the Pan:** Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Add the high smoke point oil and let it get smoking hot. You want the oil to shimmer and just begin to smoke.
3.  **Sear the Steaks:** Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot pan. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep golden-brown crust forms. If your steaks have a fat cap on the side, render it by holding the steak with tongs and searing the fat cap for about 1 minute.
4.  **Add Butter, Garlic, and Herbs:** Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the butter, minced garlic, and fresh herb sprigs to the pan. As the butter melts, tilt the pan slightly and use a spoon to continuously baste the melted garlic butter over the steaks. Continue basting for 2-4 minutes, flipping the steaks every minute or so, until they reach your desired doneness.
    *   For rare: 125-130°F (52-54°C)
    *   For medium-rare: 130-135°F (54-57°C)
    *   For medium: 135-140°F (57-60°C)
5.  **Rest the Steaks:** Once the steaks reach your desired internal temperature, remove them from the pan and place them on a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist steak.
6.  **Serve:** Slice against the grain, if desired, and spoon any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the top. Enjoy your exquisite New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter!

## Tips for Perfect Garlic Butter Steak

Achieving the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter every time involves a few key techniques:

*   **Room Temperature is Key:** Cold steaks cook unevenly, leaving the outside overcooked and the inside undercooked.
*   **Pat Dry, Always:** Moisture on the surface of the steak creates steam, preventing that beautiful crust.
*   **Hot Pan, Quick Sear:** A screaming hot pan is essential for developing a flavorful, crispy crust (the Maillard reaction) before the interior overcooks.
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** Cook one or two steaks at a time, depending on your pan size. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and steams the meat instead of searing it.
*   **Baste Generously:** The basting process is where all that glorious garlic butter flavor penetrates the steak. Don't skip it!
*   **Rest is Best:** Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, resulting in a significantly more tender and juicy steak. Don't cut into it immediately!
*   **Use a Meat Thermometer:** This is the most reliable way to ensure your steak is cooked to your preferred doneness without guessing.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Steak

A succulent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter deserves equally delicious accompaniments. For a classic American dinner, consider:

*   **Creamy Mashed Potatoes:** A perfect vehicle for soaking up the extra garlic butter.
*   **Roasted Asparagus or Green Beans:** Simple, fresh, and a great textural contrast.
*   **Creamed Spinach:** A rich and luxurious side that complements the steak beautifully.
*   **A Simple Green Salad:** A light vinaigrette can cut through the richness of the steak.
*   **Baked Potato:** Loaded with butter, sour cream, and chives.

For beverage pairings, a bold red wine like a Cabernet Sauvignon or a Merlot will complement the robust flavors of the steak.

## Recipe Variations & Expert Tips

While the classic New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is sublime on its own, a few variations can enhance your culinary adventure:

*   **Herb Power-Up:** Experiment with other fresh herbs like sage or oregano in the basting butter. A sprig of thyme and a sprig of rosemary together create a wonderful aroma.
*   **Spice Rubs:** Before searing, apply a dry rub to the steak for an extra layer of flavor. Smoked paprika, onion powder, and a touch of cayenne can add depth.
*   **Shallot Infusion:** Sauté thinly sliced shallots with the garlic for a milder, sweeter onion flavor in your butter.
*   **Deglaze with Wine:** After removing the steaks, you can add a splash of red wine or beef broth to the pan, scrape up the browned bits, and reduce it slightly to create a quick pan sauce to drizzle over the rested steak.
*   **Expert Tip: Compound Butter:** Prepare a garlic herb compound butter in advance. Simply mix softened butter with minced garlic, chopped fresh herbs, salt, and pepper. Form into a log, chill, and then slice off a pat to melt over your hot steak after it rests for an extra flavor boost.
*   **Expert Tip: Reverse Sear:** For thicker steaks (1.5 inches or more), consider reverse searing. Bake the steak in a low oven (250°F/120°C) until it reaches about 10-15°F below your desired final temperature, then sear it in a hot pan with garlic butter. This method ensures incredibly even cooking from edge to edge.

Mastering the art of New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a rewarding endeavor. With its rich flavor, tender texture, and aromatic finish, it’s a dish that truly embodies the comfort and luxury of American dinner recipes. Whether for a special occasion or a delightful weeknight treat, this recipe promises to impress and satisfy.
